title: Writing - Part 1: Teenage Stress
description: Your class has been discussing the causes of stress among teenagers and what can be done to help young people cope with pressure.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the causes in your notes. You should explain which cause you think contributes most to teenage stress, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Constant exams and homework leave young people with little time to relax or pursue their interests.
Social media creates unrealistic standards and leads to feelings of inadequacy and anxiety.
Parents and relatives sometimes place excessive pressure on teenagers to succeed academically or professionally.

structure.intro: Introduce the issue of teenage stress and state the two causes you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first cause and how it affects young people.
structure.body2: Discuss the second cause and compare its impact to the first.
structure.conclusion: State which cause you regard as most significant and suggest a way forward.

content.modelAnswer:
Stress among teenagers has become a growing concern in many societies. Young people today face a variety of pressures that previous generations did not experience to the same extent. Two of the most significant causes are academic pressure and the influence of social media.

The demands of school life, including frequent exams, assignments and the expectation of achieving top grades, create considerable stress for many young people. When academic performance is closely tied to future opportunities, teenagers may feel that failure is not an option. This constant pressure can lead to anxiety, sleep problems and in some cases, serious mental health difficulties.

Social media introduces another layer of stress by exposing young people to a carefully curated image of others' lives. Comparing themselves to seemingly perfect peers can leave teenagers feeling insecure and inadequate. Cyberbullying and the fear of missing out further compound these negative effects.

In my view, social media is the more damaging cause of teenage stress. While academic pressure exists in a defined context, social media is present constantly, even during leisure time. Encouraging young people to limit their use and develop a critical approach to online content could significantly improve their wellbeing.
